Let's think step-by-step. This page lists the issues for a GitLab project. Each issue includes information about its labels. I can see the issues and their associated labels, but some issues don't have any labels listed. I will check each issue and make a list of issues that don't have any labels.

The issues that don't have any labels are:
1. "Refactor prompt generation to not require loading a model"
2. "Validate args to prevent RuntimeError: attn_batches % batches_per_block == 0"
3. "Model_parallel=2 and 2 gpus on FAIR cluster: RuntimeError: Expected all tensors to be on the same device, but found at least two devices, cpu and cuda:1! (when checking arugment for argument index in method wrapper_index_select)"
4. "Inconsistency between valid ppl from TensorBoard and eval_lm.py"
5. "[eval] RuntimeError: attn_batches % batches_per_block when run with certain tasks"
6. "[eval] Process hanging when eval 175B model on more than 1 nodes"
7. '"RuntimeError: torch.distributed is not yet initialized but process group is requested" when trying to run API'

I believe I have achieved the objective. In summary, the next action I will perform is ```stop ["Refactor prompt generation to not require loading a model", "Validate args to prevent RuntimeError: attn_batches % batches_per_block == 0", "Model_parallel=2 and 2 gpus on FAIR cluster: RuntimeError: Expected all tensors to be on the same device, but found at least two devices, cpu and cuda:1! (when checking arugment for argument index in method wrapper_index_select)", "Inconsistency between valid ppl from TensorBoard and eval_lm.py", "[eval] RuntimeError: attn_batches % batches_per_block when run with certain tasks", "[eval]
